Mechanism: Ocean water pushed inland by cyclone pressure gradient and onshore winds; 1.6m surge at Karaikal; seawater entered villages 0.5–1 km from coast; silted irrigation canals
170mm/24hr at Thiruthuraipoondi, Muthupet; 200mm total Nagapattinam; all at once on flat delta → instant standing floods
Affected Area: All delta districts — Nagapattinam, Thiruvarur, Thanjavur

Mechanism: Cauvery delta is alluvial plain <5m MSL; drainage gradient near zero; water ponds instead of draining; even 50mm rain causes waterlogging for days
NE Monsoon 2018 had already saturated delta soils; soil moisture at field capacity → near-zero infiltration → all cyclone rain became surface runoff instantly
Grand Anicut 12-canal system overflowed simultaneously; backwater pushed into paddy fields; canals act as flood conductors into interior
Mettur + Grand Anicut + Lower Anicut released excess water to protect structures; added Cauvery flow to already-flooded channels
Storm surge acted as plug at Cauvery/Kollidam/Vettar river mouths preventing inland flood drainage; rivers backed up; reverse flow pushed floodwater further inland
Affected Area: River banks 10–20 km inland from sea

Mechanism: 7.5 million trees uprooted; fallen coconut palms, banana stems blocked irrigation channels and natural drains; water pooled instead of flowing
01:45 AM landfall in darkness; people unaware of water rising; delayed evacuation; many trapped in flooded homes
Affected Area: All coastal villages in Nagapattinam, Thiruvarur

Mechanism: 280mm rain on steep Western Ghats slopes; water-laden soil mass failures; rivers in Idukki/Ernakulam rose rapidly; roads blocked preventing rescue
